steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that a kestrel's surroundings significantly affect its hormone levels, then uses 'Therefore' to signal an expected finding. The blank must report a difference in hormone levels between wild and captive kestrels.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — compares hormone levels across different species: The passage is about wild vs. captive kestrels, not across species. ✗
  • Choice B — claims wild and captive kestrels have similar hormone levels: 'Therefore' signals that different surroundings should produce different levels — similarity contradicts the logic. ✗
  • Choice C — addresses variation among individual kestrels: Individual variation is unrelated to the environmental effect the passage describes. ✗
  • Choice D — wild males have higher androgen levels than captive males during breeding season: This reports a difference in hormone levels tied directly to setting (wild vs. captive), completing the 'Therefore' inference. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
